LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GERRIE SCOTT'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER TO SEAL RECORD. DEFENDANT GERRIE SCOTT'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER TO SEAL THE RECORD IS OFF CALENDAR. THE MOTION WAS SERVED BY MAIL ON FEBRUARY 14, 2019, AND FILED ON FEBRUARY 19, 2019, WITH THE SUPPORTING DECLARATION FILED ON FEBRUARY 20, 2019. A MOTION MUST BE SERVED AND FILED AT LEAST 16 COURT DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING. (CODE CIV. PROC. 1005(B).) FOR DEFENDANT'S GUIDANCE, THE COURT MAY ORDER THAT A RECORD BE FILED UNDER SEAL ONLY IF IT EXPRESSLY FINDS FACTS THAT ESTABLISH THAT THERE EXISTS AN OVERRIDING INTEREST THAT OVERCOMES THE RIGHT OF PUBLIC ACCESS TO THE RECORD, THE OVERRIDING INTEREST SUPPORTS SEALING THE RECORD, A SUBSTANTIAL PROBABILITY EXISTS THAT THE OVERRIDING INTEREST WILL BE PREJUDICED IF THE RECORD IS NOT SEALED, THE PROPOSED SEALING IS NARROWLY TAILORED, AND NO LESS RESTRICTIVE MEANS EXIST TO ACHIEVE THE OVERRIDING INTEREST THAT OVERCOMES THE RIGHT OF PUBLIC ACCESS TO THE RECORD. (CAL. R. CT. 2.550(D).) A COURT MAY NOT PERMIT A RECORD TO BE FILED UNDER SEAL BASED SOLELY ON THE PARTIES' STIPULATION OR AGREEMENT. (CAL. R. CT. 2.551(A).) A LITIGANT'S MERE DESIRE TO AVOID EMBARRASSMENT OR ADVERSE PUBLICITY DOES NOT CONSTITUTE AN OVERRIDING INTEREST. FURTHER, THE COURT IS EXCEEDINGLY UNLIKELY TO GRANT A REQUEST SEEKING TO SEAL AN ENTIRE COURT FILE, WHICH APPEARS TO BE THE RELIEF SOUGHT BY THE MOTION ("ANY AND ALL FILINGS IN THIS ACTION").JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR#5976, SSAWYER@SFTC.ORG, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order To Seal Records And Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Jul-02-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GERRIE SCOTT'S Motion For Protective Order To Seal Records.

Defendant Gerrie Scott's motion for protective order to seal records is denied. Defendant requests the Court to order that "the entire case record, enumerated by the Register of Actions," be sealed. However, Defendant fails to show that any of the criteria set forth in Rule 2.550(d) of the California Rules of Court are met as to any individual record in the court file, much less the entire file. In particular, she fails even to articulate any overriding privacy or other interest in any record filed in this case that overcomes the right of public access to the Court's records. Defendant's assertions that "there was a negotiated settlement agreement between the parties in this case which provided for sealing and confidentiality, and that out-of-court settlement precluded the need for a trial," are patently inadequate to satisfy the criteria of the Rule. (See, e.g., Huffy Corp. v. Superior Court (2004) 112 Cal.App.4th 97, 105-107 [reversing trial court's order sealing documents including settlement agreement with non-party which had a confidentiality provision; "before papers can be sealed, there must be substantial prejudice to an overriding interest if the documents are made available to the public."].) The mere fact that Defendant objects to having been named in a lawsuit, or would prefer that the public not be aware of that fact, is not grounds for sealing the court's file. Defendant's arguments that Rule 2.550 does not apply and, in the alternative, that her "penumbra right to privacy outweighs the public's right to know under the 1st Amendment" are meritless. Finally, unless they are attached to records in the court's file, the deposition transcripts and responses to discovery requests Defendant refers to are in the parties' possession, not the court's, and therefore cannot be "sealed" by the Court.

Counsel for Plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-22-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order To Seal Record
Off Calendar Mar-06-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GERRIE SCOTT's Motion For Protective Order To Seal Record.

Defendant Gerrie Scott's motion for a protective order to seal the record is off calendar. The motion was served by mail on February 14, 2019, and filed on February 19, 2019, with the supporting declaration filed on February 20, 2019. A motion must be served and filed at least 16 court days before the hearing. (Code Civ. Proc. 1005(b).)

For Defendant's guidance, the Court may order that a record be filed under seal only if it expressly finds facts that establish that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record, the overriding interest supports sealing the record, a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed, the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored, and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record. (Cal. R. Ct. 2.550(d).) A court may not permit a record to be filed under seal based solely on the parties' stipulation or agreement. (Cal. R. Ct. 2.551(a).) A litigant's mere desire to avoid embarrassment or adverse publicity does not constitute an overriding interest. Further, the Court is exceedingly unlikely to grant a request seeking to seal an entire court file, which appears to be the relief sought by the motion ("any and all filings in this action").

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR#5976, ssawyer@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/EPS)
